item.title: TRADING DAY Seeking signals from the noise
item.description: Wall Street and the Asian stock markets that were open ticked higher on Easter Monday, as investors shrugged off another rise in oil prices and more belligerent rhetoric from U.S. President Donald Trump on Iran, and awaited more concrete ceasefire news.

item.title: Phillips 66 says Q1 results hit by $900 million mark-to-market losses
item.description: Phillips вҖӢ66 said вҖҢon Monday вҖӢthat вҖӢits first-quarter вҒ results вҖӢwere hit вҖӢby nearly $900 million вҖӢin вҖӢpre-tax mark-to-market вҖҢlosses, вҒ as a sharp вҖӢrise вҖӢin вҒ commodity prices вҖӢweighed вҖӢon вҒ the вҒ refiner.

item.title: US finalizes 2.48% Medicare Advantage payment hike; insurance stocks surge
item.description: The U.S. government has finalized a 2.48% average increase вҖӢin payments to private insurers offering Medicare вҖҢAdvantage plans to older adults in 2027, a much steeper rise than the near-flat increase it proposed in вҖӢJanuary.

item.title: CBS to sell late-night hours to Byron Allen as Colbert show ends
item.description: CBS said on Monday it was turning its 11:35 p.m. ET post-local-news timeslot into a time buy, selling it вҖӢto Byron Allen, after the end of Stephen Colbert's вҖҢlate-night program in May.

item.title: Nvidia acquisition of SchedMD sparks worry among AI specialists about software access
item.description: A niche acquisition by Nvidia has raised concerns among artificial-intelligence and supercomputer specialists who see the move as a test of the biggest AI chip company's commitment to maintaining вҖӢa fair playing field for chip rivals and AI data center builders.

item.title: Exclusive: Amazon says it has reached deal with US Postal Service on package deliveries
item.description: Amazon.com said Monday вҖӢit has reached a new agreement with вҖҢthe U.S. Postal Service on package deliveries.

item.title: War in Middle East will lead to slower growth, higher inflation, IMF chief tells Reuters
item.description: The war in the Middle East will lead to higher inflation вҖӢand slower global growth, the head of вҖҢthe International Monetary Fund told Reuters on Monday, ahead of a fresh forecast for the world economy вҖӢplanned by the global lender for next вҖӢweek.

item.title: Netflix debuts new 'Playground' gaming app for kids
item.description: Netflix doubled down on its gaming efforts on Monday, launching a new app called "Netflix Playground" that would feature games built вҖӢaround popular children's characters such as Peppa Pig and Sesame Street.

item.title: Maker of Stanley tumblers prevails in lawsuit over lead scare
item.description: A federal вҖӢjudge dismissed a proposed class-action lawsuit accusing the maker of Stanley tumblers вҖҢof concealing that the popular water bottles contained lead.

item.title: Trump proposes to cut 9,400 TSA workers, $1.5 billion from budget
item.description: The White House is proposing to вҖӢcut more than 9,400 вҖҢworkers and just over $1.5 billion from the 60,000-employee Transportation вҖӢSecurity Administration that вҖӢhandles airport security operations, according вҒ to budget documents.

item.title: Fed's Goolsbee, Hammack say inflation is flashing 'orange,' or worse
item.description: Cleveland Federal Reserve President Beth Hammack and Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee both see inflation as a far bigger problem than employment, underscoring their support for tighter rather than looser monetary policy вҖӢas the Iran war puts upward pressure on energy prices and the job market remains вҖҢstuck in low gear.

item.title: FAA proposes to hire 2,300 air traffic controllers in budget request
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration said Monday вҖӢit is proposing вҖҢto hire 2,300 air traffic controller trainees вҖӢas it works вҖӢto address a persistent вҒ lack of personnel.

item.title: White House says increased productivity means Fed can cut rates
item.description: White House economic вҖҢadviser Kevin Hassett told CNBC on Monday that he believes a "supply вҖӢshock" in the U.S. вҖӢeconomy caused by capital spending вҒ and higher productivity from artificial intelligence will вҖӢallow the Federal Reserve to вҖӢlower interest rates.

item.title: NY Fed says supply chain pressures heated up in March
item.description: Supply chain pressures heated up in March to вҖӢlevels last seen at the вҖҢstart of 2023, data released by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York said вҖӢMonday.

item.title: Treasury designates BNY as financial agent for Trump accounts
item.description: The вҖӢBank of New York вҖҢMellon Corporation has been chose as a financial agent of the вҖӢU.S. government to support implementation вҖӢof the new Trump Accounts program, вҒ the U.S. Treasury Department вҖӢsaid on Monday.

item.title: Brazil's Azul appoints ex-Embraer CFO Garcia in finance reshuffle
item.description: Brazilian airline Azul said вҖҢon Monday that Chief Financial Officer Alex вҖӢMalfitani will step вҖӢdown on April 20, with Embraer's вҒ Antonio Carlos Garcia set вҖӢto assume the role.

item.title: US service sector cools in March; price paid measure highest in 3-1/2 years
item.description: U.S. services sector growth slowed in March, while prices paid by вҖҢbusinesses for inputs climbed to near a 3-1/2-year high, an early sign that the prolonged war with Iran was boosting inflation pressures.

item.title: New Jersey cannot regulate Kalshi's prediction market, US appeals court rules
item.description: A federal appeals court on вҖӢMonday ruled that New вҖҢJersey gaming regulators cannot prevent Kalshi from вҖӢallowing people вҖӢin the state to use вҒ its prediction вҖӢmarket to place financial вҖӢbets on the outcome of sporting events.

item.title: Barings' private credit fund limits withdrawals after redemption requests surge
item.description: A private credit fund managed by Barings capped withdrawals at 5% of shares after a вҖӢsurge in redemption requests, the latest in a series of similar вҖҢmoves by asset managers in recent months.

item.title: BlackRock files for Nasdaq-100 fund, expanding competition with Invesco
item.description: BlackRock has filed for an exchange-traded fund that will track the Nasdaq-100 , in a challenge вҖӢto Invesco's dominance in a market where вҖҢonly a handful of funds directly follow the tech-heavy index.

item.title: Brazil's Embraer CFO Garcia resigns, CEO Gomes Neto to take interim role
item.description: Brazilian вҖҢplanemaker Embraer вҖӢsaid вҖӢon Monday that вҒ CFO вҖӢAntonio вҖӢCarlos Garcia has вҖӢresigned вҖӢand CEO Francisco вҖҢGomes вҒ Neto will assume вҖӢthe вҖӢposition вҒ on вҖӢan вҖӢinterim вҒ basis.

item.title: Investors press Amazon, Microsoft and Google on water, power use in US data centers
item.description: Amazon, Microsoft and Alphabet's Google have each recently abandoned construction of multibillion-dollar data centers over community opposition and now the companies вҖӢare coming under shareholder pressure over the environmental impact of their projects.

item.title: National Healthcare Properties moves closer to public markets with US IPO filing
item.description: Real estate investment trust вҖӢNational Healthcare Properties on вҖҢMonday made public its paperwork for an вҖӢinitial public offering вҖӢin the United States.

item.title: Oracle appoints Hilary Maxson as CFO
item.description: Oracle said вҖӢon Monday it вҖҢhad appointed Hilary Maxson as chief financial officer, effective вҖӢimmediately.

item.title: US equity funds see a second successive weekly inflow
item.description: U.S. equity funds witnessed substantial inflows in the seven days to April 1 as worries вҖӢover the Middle East war eased temporarily after вҖҢPresident Donald Trump indicated that the United States was nearing the completion of its objectives for the war.

item.title: Goldman Sachs private credit fund defies redemption surge across industry
item.description: Goldman Sachs' private credit fund said on Monday investors requested to repurchase just under 5% of shares in the first quarter, staying within its cap and defying a broader rise in redemption requests across the вҖӢsector.

item.title: Neurocrine to buy Soleno Therapeutics for $2.9 billion
item.description: Neurocrine Biosciences will acquire rare disease drugmaker вҖӢSoleno Therapeutics for $2.9 billion in вҖҢcash, the companies said on Monday, marking the neuroscience-focused drugmaker's expansion into вҖӢmetabolic disorders.

item.title: Madison Air targets $13.2 billion valuation in US IPO
item.description: Ventilation and вҖҢfiltration systems provider Madison Air Solutions said on вҖӢMonday it was вҖӢtargeting a valuation of вҒ up to $13.2 billion вҖӢin its initial public вҖӢoffering in the United States.

item.title: JPMorgan's Dimon warns Iran war may drive inflation and interest rates higher
item.description: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon warned on Monday that the war in Iran risks oil and commodity вҖӢprice shocks that could keep inflation sticky and push interest rates higher than the market now expects.

item.title: US crude premiums climb to record levels as Asia, Europe compete for supply
item.description: Spot premiums for U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude have jumped вҖӢto all-time highs as competition between Asian and European refiners for supply heats up вҖҢto replace Middle Eastern oil flows disrupted by the Iran war, industry sources said.
